Finding: API key hardcoded in wrangler.toml
Notes: PROJECTHUB_API_KEY committed in plain text

Finding: Unauthenticated membership invite endpoint
Notes: Any caller could trigger Hub API invitations with no auth

Finding: Unverified webhook triggers privileged invite
Notes: No secret verification before processing donations

Finding: Webhook secret exposed in URL query param
Notes: v2 fix used ?secret=TOKEN — logged by Cloudflare/proxy access logs. Moved to X-Webhook-Secret header

Finding: Timing attack on token comparison
Notes: !== operator used for secret comparison — replaced with timingSafeEqual

Finding: Secret key logged to browser console
Notes: console.log("PUBLIC_SECRET_KEY:"

Finding: Full donor PII payload echoed in HTTP response
Notes: Raw payload including donor email returned in webhook response body

Finding: Middleware cached POST/API responses as public
Notes: Cache-Control set to public max-age=600 for all responses including API

Finding: No rate limiting on any endpoint
Notes: Cloudflare WAF rate limiting rules applied and confirmed live (HTTP 429 verified)

Finding: Wildcard CORS on all endpoints including write endpoints
Notes: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on POST endpoints removed

Finding: membership-invite proxied arbitrary body fields to Hub API
Notes: Only whitelisted fields (email/type/paymentReference) now forwarded

Finding: No CSRF/Origin check on form submission endpoints
Notes: Origin header check added — returns 403 for non-techforpalestine.org origins

Finding: No input format or length validation on forms
Notes: Email regex; URL via new URL(); 2000 char limit on all text fields

Finding: getEnv globalThis fallback allowed client-side secret resolution
Notes: globalThis[name] fallback resolved to window[name] in browser context

Finding: Donor PII (full email) written to server logs
Notes: All log calls now redact to [redacted]@domain.com

Finding: Internal error.message leaked in HTTP response bodies
Notes: catch blocks returned raw error messages to clients

Finding: Dual middleware files — cache-control layer was dead code
Notes: Astro loaded src/middleware.ts only — index.ts never ran. Refactored to sequence(cacheControl csp) with separate files

Finding: PUBLIC_SECRET_KEY in client-side Axios Authorization header bundle
Notes: Secret was bundled into browser JS via Axios instance headers. Replaced with server-side proxy

Finding: CSP script-src used 'unsafe-inline' defeating XSS protection
Notes: Per-request nonce via crypto.randomUUID() + HTMLRewriter + strict-dynamic

Finding: project-proxy forwarded Authorization to any upstream path
Notes: Only leading / was checked — any upstream endpoint reachable with auth. Path now must start with /api/method/

Finding: project-proxy dot-segment traversal bypassed /api/method/ prefix check
Notes: /api/method/../../admin passed prefix check but fetch() normalised to /admin. Fixed with new URL() normalisation before guard

Finding: Notion DB IDs committed in wrangler.toml
Notes: Database IDs moved to Cloudflare Pages dashboard environment variables

Finding: QGiv embed script loaded without Subresource Integrity hash
OWASP: A08:2021 – Software and Data Integrity Failures
Notes: No integrity attribute on dynamically injected script tag — supply chain risk

Finding: Notion image proxy worker had no authentication
Notes: Worker removed entirely in PR #415 — finding closed

Finding: style-src 'unsafe-inline' in CSP remained after script-src was nonce-protected
Notes: Nonce applied to style-src; HTMLRewriter extended to inject nonce into <style> tags

Finding: project-proxy forwarded all browser request headers to upstream including Cookie and X-Forwarded-For
Notes: Replaced header copy-all with explicit FORWARD_HEADERS allowlist (content-type/accept/accept-language/accept-encoding only)
